hero.eyebrow: Huashu-inspired integration for real MCP systems
hero.title: A source-synced design surface for orchestrator-mcp and minimax-mcp
hero.summary: This showcase turns the live MCP servers into a polished explainer, router laboratory, and printable artifact — without inventing features that the code does not actually ship.
principles: Source-of-truth generation from server code and configs
Single HTML surface for docs, demo, and handoff
Verification gates that fail when the showcase drifts

stats.label: Consensus-ready scenarios
stats.detail: 1 high-risk examples route into stronger governance

narrativePanels.title: Source first
narrativePanels.body: The showcase is generated from the MCP servers and configs themselves, so the docs stay anchored to real tool surfaces instead of drifting marketing copy.

narrativePanels.title: One HTML surface
narrativePanels.body: The same static HTML can serve as onboarding page, architecture explainer, live router lab, and printable handoff artifact.

narrativePanels.title: Verify before claiming
narrativePanels.body: Tests fail when server capabilities change without the showcase being rebuilt, which keeps the design layer honest.

quickstartRecipes.title: Preflight a task before execution
quickstartRecipes.intent: See the inferred task type, primary model, fallback chain, memory mode, and consensus mode without spending execution tokens.

args.task: Investigate why consensus hangs on security-sensitive architecture reviews across multiple files

quickstartRecipes.title: Let the router pick the lane
quickstartRecipes.intent: Hand the problem to the router with an explicit operation mode and optional memory recall.

args.task: Review this authentication patch for privilege escalation risks and propose the safest fix

quickstartRecipes.title: Ask for consensus when stakes are high
quickstartRecipes.intent: Fan out to multiple models and synthesize disagreement instead of trusting a single answer.

args.prompt: Should we favor direct model calls or routed orchestration for debugging flaky CI failures?

quickstartRecipes.title: Pull in MiniMax as a fast contrast model
quickstartRecipes.intent: Use MiniMax as a lightweight alternate lens for debugging or code review.

args.content: Option A: send every task through the router. Option B: allow direct model pinning for low-risk lookups.
args.context: We want speed without losing observability.

routerLab.promptPlaceholder: Paste a task here to see how the router would classify it...

single: single file|one file|one function|one class|this file

examples.title: Security investigation
examples.prompt: Investigate an auth bypass vulnerability across multiple services and recommend the safest remediation plan.

examples.title: Fast lookup
examples.prompt: Find files mentioning session_info and list the handlers that return it.

examples.title: Architecture decision
examples.prompt: Design a multi-model orchestration strategy for long-context debugging with fallback, memory recall, and audit trails.

examples.title: Debug regression
examples.prompt: Debug why session compression causes stale summaries after repeated context truncation.

scenarioRoutes.title: Security investigation
scenarioRoutes.prompt: Investigate an auth bypass vulnerability across multiple services and recommend the safest remediation plan.
scenarioRoutes.why: High risk, cross-file work should trigger a security-first route and a strong consensus posture.

route.reasoning: Intent inferred as security. Risk=high, scope=cross-file, depth=deep. Memory mode hybrid selected for security workflow. Consensus mode strong enabled. Primary model gpt54 chosen for reasoning=frontier, structuredOutputs=high, latency=medium

scenarioRoutes.title: Fast lookup
scenarioRoutes.prompt: Find files mentioning session_info and list the handlers that return it.
scenarioRoutes.why: A small search should stay cheap, fast, and single-shot.

route.reasoning: Intent inferred as search. Risk=low, scope=cross-file, depth=fast. Memory mode exact selected for search workflow. Primary model minimax chosen for reasoning=medium, structuredOutputs=medium, latency=fast. Primary model is in the speed tier

scenarioRoutes.title: Architecture decision
scenarioRoutes.prompt: Design a multi-model orchestration strategy for long-context debugging with fallback, memory recall, and audit trails.
scenarioRoutes.why: This is where the router should prefer deep reasoning and a stronger model chain.

route.reasoning: Intent inferred as debug. Risk=medium, scope=cross-file, depth=balanced. Memory mode hybrid selected for debug workflow. Consensus mode light enabled. Primary model claude45 chosen for reasoning=frontier, structuredOutputs=high, latency=medium

scenarioRoutes.title: Debug regression
scenarioRoutes.prompt: Debug why session compression causes stale summaries after repeated context truncation.
scenarioRoutes.why: A real bug hunt should pick a deeper lane but keep consensus lighter than a security review.

route.reasoning: Intent inferred as debug. Risk=medium, scope=cross-file, depth=balanced. Memory mode hybrid selected for debug workflow. Consensus mode light enabled. Primary model claude45 chosen for reasoning=frontier, structuredOutputs=high, latency=medium

tools.name: call_model
tools.description: Directly call a specific model without routing or fallback. Use when you know exactly which model you want.
tools.highlight: Directly call a specific model without routing or fallback

operation.description: Optional operation override for action-aware security handling

tools.name: orchestrate
tools.description: Auto-route a task to the best model with automatic fallback. Analyzes the task, selects the optimal model based on task type, and executes with fallback through the model chain if needed. Optionally includes Mem0 recall for context.
tools.highlight: One entrypoint for routing, fallback, memory recall, and execution.

task.description: The task description or prompt to execute

content.description: Optional content to analyze (code, text, etc.)

task_type.description: Force a specific task type instead of auto-detecting

use_memory.description: Include Mem0 recall for relevant context (default: false)

operation.description: Optional operation override for action-aware security handling

tools.name: route_explain
tools.description: Explain how a task would be routed without executing it. Shows multi-axis routing details, selected model, fallback chain, memory mode, consensus mode, and reasoning.
tools.highlight: Shows exactly how the router would think before any token spend.

task.description: The task to analyze routing for

tools.name: consensus
tools.description: Query multiple models in parallel, detect disagreements, and build consensus. Use for important decisions that benefit from multiple perspectives. Optionally includes Mem0 recall.
tools.highlight: Runs a model council when the question deserves more than one mind.

prompt.description: The prompt to send to all models

models.description: Models to query (default: auto-selected based on task)

task_type.description: Task type to auto-select models if not specified

use_memory.description: Include Mem0 recall for relevant context (default: false)

operation.description: Optional operation override for action-aware security handling

tools.name: reasoning_loop
tools.description: Execute iterative reasoning with RDT-inspired architecture (Recurrent-Depth Transformer). Loops through analysis with ACT-based convergence detection, LTI-stable state management, and depth-based model selection. Use for complex problems requiring deep multi-iteration analysis.
tools.highlight: Turns deep reasoning into an auditable loop instead of a black box.

task.description: The problem or task to reason about deeply

max_loops.description: Maximum reasoning iterations (default: auto-inferred from task complexity, range 2-8)

mode.description: Reasoning depth: quick (3 loops), balanced (auto), deep (8 loops with consensus)

use_consensus.description: Use multi-model consensus on alternating iterations (default: false)

early_halt.description: Enable ACT-based early halting when confidence converges (default: true)

system.description: Optional system prompt for all iterations

tools.name: vote
tools.description: Quick yes/no vote across multiple models. Returns decision based on majority.
tools.highlight: Quick yes/no vote across multiple models

question.description: The yes/no question to vote on

tools.name: get_prompt
tools.description: Get the layered prompt template for a task type and model family. Includes identity, operating policy, task lens, style adapter, and output contract.
tools.highlight: Get the layered prompt template for a task type and model family

model.description: Model to get prompt for (determines mechanics vs principles)

context.description: Content to include in the prompt

compare.description: If true, return both mechanics and principles versions for comparison

tools.name: compress_context
tools.description: Compress context using 3-layer compression (snip → semantic → summary) to fit token limits.
tools.highlight: Compress context using 3-layer compression (snip → semantic → summary) to fit token limits

tools.name: mem0_recall
tools.description: Search Mem0 for relevant memories before analysis. Use to recall past bugs, fixes, patterns, and learned context.
tools.highlight: Search Mem0 for relevant memories before analysis

query.description: Search query (bug type, error message, file name, symptom)

tools.name: mem0_store
tools.description: Store a fix pattern or learning to Mem0 for future reference.
tools.highlight: Store a fix pattern or learning to Mem0 for future reference

content.description: The memory content to store (bug type, root cause, fix approach, etc.)

bug_type.description: Type of bug (optional, for structured storage)

tools.name: session_info
tools.description: Get current session info including transcript length, model usage, tokens, and costs.
tools.highlight: Get current session info including transcript length, model usage, tokens, and costs

tools.name: axon_context
tools.description: Get 360-degree context for a symbol: callers, callees, dependencies, usages.
tools.highlight: Get 360-degree context for a symbol: callers, callees, dependencies, usages

tools.name: axon_dead_code
tools.description: Find dead/unused code in the repository.
tools.highlight: Find dead/unused code in the repository

tools.name: axon_impact
tools.description: Get blast radius analysis: what would be affected if this symbol changes.
tools.highlight: Get blast radius analysis: what would be affected if this symbol changes

tools.name: axon_query
tools.description: Search the Axon knowledge graph for code symbols, relationships, and patterns.
tools.highlight: Search the Axon knowledge graph for code symbols, relationships, and patterns

tools.name: multifix_analyze
tools.description: Full multifix workflow: Mem0 recall → Axon system map → Multi-model consensus → Cost report. Use for bug fixing with memory-enhanced analysis.
tools.highlight: Full multifix workflow: Mem0 recall → Axon system map → Multi-model consensus → Cost report

bug_description.description: Description of the bug or issue

code_context.description: Relevant code, stack traces, or system map

tools.name: posthog_guide
tools.description: Get guidance on using PostHog MCP tools for analytics, experiments, feature flags, and insights.
tools.highlight: Get guidance on using PostHog MCP tools for analytics, experiments, feature flags, and insights

topic.description: PostHog topic to get guidance on

tools.name: squirrel_audit
tools.description: Audit a website for SEO, performance, security, accessibility, and 15+ other categories with 230+ rules.
tools.highlight: Audit a website for SEO, performance, security, accessibility, and 15+ other categories with 230+ rules

coverage.description: Coverage mode: quick (fast), surface (one per pattern), full (comprehensive)

tools.name: get_security_status
tools.description: Get current security status: permission mode, trust levels, recent denials, session metrics.
tools.highlight: Get current security status: permission mode, trust levels, recent denials, session metrics

tools.name: set_permission_mode
tools.description: Set the permission mode for model calls. Modes: default (interactive), plan (read-only), auto (auto-approve safe ops), bypass (skip all checks).
tools.highlight: Set the permission mode for model calls

tools.name: validate_prompt
tools.description: Validate a prompt for security issues without executing. Returns issues found and severity.
tools.highlight: Applies security policy before risky prompts cross trust boundaries.

model.description: Model to validate against (affects trust level)

literal_mode.description: If true, treat dangerous-looking strings as code/data literals instead of executable intent

tools.name: cost_report
tools.description: Get usage and cost summary for all models in this session. Shows calls, tokens, and costs per model.
tools.highlight: Surfaces spend and token usage so orchestration stays economically honest.

reset.description: Reset usage tracking after generating report

tools.name: model_info
tools.description: Get information about available models, costs, and fallback chains.
tools.highlight: Get information about available models, costs, and fallback chains

model.description: Specific model to get info about (optional)

tools.name: tool_status
tools.description: Check availability of all integrated tools (Axon, Squirrel, Mem0, PostHog, models).
tools.highlight: Check availability of all integrated tools (Axon, Squirrel, Mem0, PostHog, models)

tools.name: minimax_chat
tools.description: Send a message to MiniMax M2.7 model and get a response. Use this to get a second opinion, compare approaches, or leverage MiniMax's reasoning capabilities alongside Claude.
tools.highlight: Fast secondary model access for quick triangulation and comparison.

prompt.description: The message/prompt to send to MiniMax

system.description: Optional system prompt to set context

include_thinking.description: Whether to include thinking/reasoning in output (default: true)

tools.name: minimax_analyze
tools.description: Have MiniMax analyze code, text, or problems. Good for getting a different perspective on complex issues.
tools.highlight: A dedicated second-opinion lane for debugging, review, and explanation.

content.description: The content to analyze (code, text, problem description)

toolSpotlights.name: orchestrate
toolSpotlights.highlight: One entrypoint for routing, fallback, memory recall, and execution.
toolSpotlights.description: Auto-route a task to the best model with automatic fallback. Analyzes the task, selects the optimal model based on task type, and executes with fallback through the model chain if needed. Optionally includes Mem0 recall for context.

toolSpotlights.description: Query multiple models in parallel, detect disagreements, and build consensus. Use for important decisions that benefit from multiple perspectives. Optionally includes Mem0 recall.

Loops through analysis with ACT-based convergence detection, LTI-stable state management, and depth-based model selection. Use for complex problems requiring deep multi-iteration analysis.

integrationNotes.title: What this adds to the workspace
bullets: A live router sandbox you can use to explain and debug routing choices before invoking models.
A presentation-grade HTML artifact for demos, onboarding, architecture reviews, and printable handoffs.
A source digest and stale-file tests so the design layer cannot silently drift away from the MCP implementation.

integrationNotes.title: How MiniMax fits
bullets: MiniMax remains a distinct fast lane, but the showcase frames it inside the broader orchestration story instead of leaving it as an isolated server.
The recipes and tool cards make the second-opinion workflow visible to operators and contributors.
